chief_complaint: A 64-year-old woman presented with altered mental status after a COVID-19 infection.
history: The patient was admitted to the emergency department due to altered mental status. She had previously been infected with COVID-19. Throughout her hospitalization, she exhibited elevated blood pressure necessitating intravenous treatment. She developed ascending symmetrical limb weakness and progressive loss of reflexes, along with a severe motor deficit and gait disturbance, accompanied by arterial blood pressure fluctuations and other signs of autonomic dysfunction.
exam: Vital signs: elevated blood pressure. Physical examination findings: bilateral occipital diffuse hypodensity, T2-weighted image hyperintensity at the parietal and occipital levels, indicative of vasogenic edema.
labs: Brain CT: bilateral occipital diffuse hypodensity. Brain MRI: T2-weighted image hyperintensity at the parietal and occipital levels, indicative of vasogenic edema. ECG: not mentioned. Bloods: euvolemic hyponatremia with concurrent low serum osmolality and high urine osmolality and sodium, indicating a syndrome of inappropriate antidiuretic hormone (SIADH).
correct_diagnosis: Guillain-Barre Syndrome (GBS) with dysautonomia triggered by a COVID-19 infection
differentials: Guillain-Barre Syndrome (GBS) with dysautonomia triggered by a COVID-19 infection
correct_ranking: Guillain-Barre Syndrome (GBS) with dysautonomia triggered by a COVID-19 infection
